channel.title: KillBait RSS Feed
channel.description: KillBait - News highlights delivered clearly and responsibly—no clickbait, no sensationalism

item.title: Murang’a Man Survives After Being Mistaken for Dead, Turns Life Around
item.description: Gabriel Macharia, a young man from Murang’a, has shared his terrifying experience of being taken to a mortuary while still alive. Macharia's troubles began in secondary school, where a strike disrupte...

item.title: Farm Fresh advances to PVL semis for the first time after eliminating Nxled
item.description: Farm Fresh made history by clinching its first-ever semifinals berth in the Premier Volleyball League (PVL) All-Filipino Conference, after defeating Nxled in a thrilling match. This marks a significan...

item.title: UK Student Loan Interest Rate Capped at 6% Amid Inflation Concerns
item.description: The UK government has announced a cap on interest rates for student loans (Plan 2 and Plan 3) at 6% from September 2026, a move intended to mitigate potential increases in inflation stemming from the ...

item.title: Escalating US and Israeli strikes target Iranian infrastructure before Trump’s ultimatum
item.description: Tensions between the United States, Israel, and Iran sharply escalated as American and Israeli forces launched extensive strikes across Iran ahead of a deadline set by Donald Trump. The former US pres...

item.title: Fremantle coach outlines plan to curb Nick Daicos ahead of key AFL clash
item.description: Fremantle coach Justin Longmuir has revealed that the Dockers have devised a detailed strategy to counter the influence of Collingwood’s Nick Daicos in their upcoming AFL matchup. Known for his quick ...

item.title: Tensions escalate between US and Iran over Strait of Hormuz blockade
item.description: Tensions between the United States and Iran have sharply escalated as Iran refuses to comply with President Donald Trump's ultimatum to reopen the Strait of Hormuz. Trump set a deadline for the strait...

item.title: Assam Police Searches Pawan Khera's Delhi Residence Amid Allegations Against CM Himanta Biswa Sarma
item.description: On 7th April 2026, Assam Police conducted a search at Congress leader Pawan Khera's residence in Delhi regarding allegations he had made against Assam Chief Minister Himanta Biswa Sarma and his family...

item.title: Violence erupts in Manipur as angry mob attacks paramilitary camp, several injured
item.description: Manipur has been facing heightened tension after an angry mob stormed a paramilitary camp, resulting in violent clashes and several injuries. The situation escalated when the mob, enraged by unresolve...

item.title: Rising Fuel Costs Push Canadian Airlines to Increase Fares
item.description: Canadians booking flights in the coming weeks could face higher prices as global fuel costs climb. The increase in airfares is due to the rising cost of jet fuel, exacerbated by the closure of the Str...

item.title: Tinubu Announces Plans to Upgrade Nigeria Police with Better Training and Modern Equipment
item.description: President Bola Tinubu talk say government wan invest wella for Nigeria Police Force to make dem world-class. For National Police Day for Abuja, Tinubu, wey his VP Kashim Shettima represent am, yan say...

item.title: Fremantle coach outlines strategy to limit Nick Daicos' influence
item.description: Fremantle Dockers coach Justin Longmuir has confirmed that his side has devised a clear game plan to combat the influence of Collingwood star Nick Daicos ahead of their upcoming AFL clash. Daicos, kno...

item.title: Youth group criticizes 'Safer Cities' program, DILG defends safety measures
item.description: A youth group, the Samahan ng Progresibong Kabataan (SPARK), has criticized the government's 'Safer Cities' initiative, calling it ‘anti-poor’ and suggesting that it may lead to abuse, especially in t...

item.title: President Kiir replaces South Sudan Parliament leaders amid corruption allegations
item.description: President Salva Kiir of South Sudan has dismissed the Speaker of Parliament Jemma Nunu Kumba and her deputy, Permena Awerial Aluong, following a decree that was read out before the Transitional Nation...

item.title: Ladysmith Black Mambazo and Family Share Funeral and Memorial Details for Albert Mazibuko
item.description: Ladysmith Black Mambazo, together with the family of Albert Mazibuko, has expressed heartfelt gratitude to the South African public, media, and supporters for their messages of love, prayers, and cond...

item.title: CPD urges policy focus on human capital as Philippine birth rate falls to 1.7
item.description: The Commission on Population and Development (CPD) has urged the Philippine government to channel more investments into education, health, and skills development following a continued decline in the n...

item.title: Murang'a Seal Coach Osbourne Monday Optimistic Despite Winless Streak
item.description: Murang'a Seal head coach, Osbourne Monday, has expressed confidence that his team will find their scoring form despite a tough patch of four matches without a win. The team recently lost 2-0 to AFC Le...

item.title: FIFA Approves Arthur Okonkwo’s Switch to Nigeria Ahead of Summer Friendlies
item.description: FIFA don finally approve Arthur Okonkwo to play for Nigeria national team, marking big move for the Super Eagles before their next international games. The Arsenal academy product wey don represent En...

item.title: New Brunswick plans toll at Nova Scotia boundary, raising concerns over trade barriers
item.description: The New Brunswick government has announced plans to implement a toll on out-of-province vehicles near its southern boundary with Nova Scotia by 2028. This highway levy, included in the 2026 provincial...

item.title: Andrew Leigh encourages Canberra staffers to reduce travel and use buses to conserve fuel
item.description: As Canberra prepares for the federal budget week, concerns about fuel shortages are influencing how staffers and lobbyists travel to the capital. Assistant Minister for Treasury and Competition, Andre...

item.title: Wouter Basson to Face HPCSA Disciplinary Hearing Over Apartheid-Era Chemical Weapons Allegations
item.description: Dr Wouter Basson, infamously known as 'Dr Teflon' for evading accountability for decades, will face a disciplinary hearing with the Health Professions Council of South Africa (HPCSA). The 75-year-old ...
